title: GetGraphHealth
description: Hosts (`graph stats`, `project status --graph`, SDK snapshot builders) need graph statistics plus VCS staleness and derivation-fingerprint diagnostics without reimplementing orchestration in each adapter. `GetGraphHealth` centralizes lock assertion, statistics retrieval, VCS ref resolution, and fingerprint comparison into one application use case that operates on an already-open `CodeGraphProvider`.
context: Hosts (`graph stats`, `project status --graph`, SDK snapshot builders) need graph statistics plus VCS staleness and derivation-fingerprint diagnostics without reimplementing orchestration in each adapter. `GetGraphHealth` centralizes lock assertion, statistics retrieval, VCS ref resolution, and fingerprint comparison into one application use case that operates on an already-open `CodeGraphProvider`.

rules:
`GetGraphHealth.execute(input)` MUST return `GetGraphHealthResult` containing all `GraphStatistics` fields plus:

- `stale: boolean | null` — VCS staleness per `code-graph:staleness-detection`
- `currentRef: string | null` — current VCS ref from `createVcsAdapter(config.projectRoot)`, or `null` when unavailable
- `fingerprintMismatch: boolean | null` — derivation mismatch when workspaces and `codeGraphVersion` allow comparison, else `null`

`GetGraphHealth` MUST rely on `provider.getStatistics()` for graph availability checks.

The use case MUST NOT call externally exposed lock helpers or accept a caller-controlled lock-assertion escape hatch.

If the provider reports that the graph is busy or stale, the use case MUST let that provider error propagate unchanged to the caller.

`GetGraphHealth` MUST resolve the current VCS ref via `createVcsAdapter` and apply `isGraphStale(lastIndexedRef, currentRef)` from staleness detection. Unknown staleness (`lastIndexedRef` is `null`) MUST yield `stale: null`, not `true`.

When `input.workspaces` is provided and `stats.graphFingerprint` is not `null`, `GetGraphHealth` MUST parse the stored fingerprint map, build the effective graph config from `input.config`, and call `detectFingerprintMismatch` with `input.codeGraphVersion`, `config.projectRoot`, workspaces, and graph config. When comparison cannot run, `fingerprintMismatch` MUST be `null`.

rules.requirement: Accepts open provider and project inputs
rules:
`GetGraphHealthInput` MUST include:

- `config: SpecdConfig`
- `provider: CodeGraphProvider` (already opened)
- `codeGraphVersion: string`
- optional workspaces for fingerprint comparison

The use case MUST NOT open or close the provider.
`createGetGraphHealth()` in composition MUST return a stateless `GetGraphHealth` instance with no config capture — all inputs arrive per `execute()` call.

constraints: MUST NOT mutate the graph store or trigger indexing.
MUST NOT load change entities or compile project context.

scenarios.name: Fresh graph with matching fingerprint
given: an opened provider with indexed data at the current VCS ref and matching derivation fingerprint
when: GetGraphHealth.execute() is called with workspaces and codeGraphVersion
then: the result includes all GraphStatistics fields

scenarios.name: Unknown staleness when no indexed ref
Busy provider error propagates unchanged
given: the provider reports GRAPH_BUSY from getStatistics()
then: the same busy error propagates to the caller

scenarios.name: Stale provider error propagates unchanged
given: the provider reports GRAPH_PROVIDER_STALE from getStatistics()
then: the same stale-provider error propagates to the caller

scenarios.name: Stale by VCS ref drift
given: lastIndexedRef differs from the current VCS ref
then: AND currentRef matches the adapter ref

scenarios.name: Mismatch detected
given: stored graphFingerprint differs from current workspace layout

scenarios.requirement: Accepts open provider and project inputs
scenarios.name: Does not open or close provider
given: a mock provider with spied open and close
then: open and close are not called

scenarios.name: Factory returns stateless instance
then: each call returns a new GetGraphHealth instance with no captured config
